title: Colysis × simplicifrons
taxonomy: Colysis × simplicifrons is a nothospecies of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a hybrid of Colysis simulans and possibly another species in the genus Colysis. The exact classification of this hybrid can be complex, and it may require further research to determine its exact parentage and classification. Colysis × simplicifrons is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and the Philippines. Its classification as a hybrid within the Polypodiaceae family is a key aspect of its botanical identity.

intro: Colysis × simplicifrons is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Colysis × simplicifrons include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Colysis × simplicifrons is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat respiratory conditions and other health issues.
